thesis: Sony Group Corporation is a high-quality Japanese conglomerate being mispriced near 52-week lows. While the market focuses on the Sony Financial spin-off charge and gaming hardware softness, the dominant Imaging & Sensing Solutions business (CMOS image sensors, camera modules, and AI-perception silicon) offers a unique call option on AI/robotics/autonomous systems. The ADR trades at a meaningful discount to analyst targets and offers strong free cash flow, recurring music/pictures IP, and a newly expanded share buyback.

drivers: Accelerated image-sensor design wins in automotive, industrial, and robotics perception
PlayStation services and content monetization stabilize gaming profits
Strong music and pictures performance drives recurring IP cash flow
Yen weakness boosts dollar-reported earnings and repatriation value
Expanded buyback and dividend execution exceed expectations

base_case.assumptions: Imaging & Sensing Solutions grows steadily while gaming transitions; entertainment cash flows remain resilient; the Sony Financial spin-off completes without major disruption; and the ADR re-rates toward analyst consensus as near-term concerns fade.

risks: Image-sensor share loss to Samsung, Omnivision, or Chinese suppliers
JPY/FX headwinds and persistent conglomerate discount
Auto/semiconductor cycle weakness delaying sensor ramp

catalysts.event: Quarterly results expected to show imaging and entertainment momentum, gaming transition progress, and any guidance updates.
Strong first-half results could lead to full-year guidance upgrades, particularly in Imaging & Sensing and Pictures.
New automotive, industrial, and robotics CMOS image-sensor design wins expand TAM and validate the AI-perception thesis.
Recurring revenue from PlayStation Plus, digital games, and live-service titles stabilizes gaming segment profitability.
Completion of the financial services separation simplifies the conglomerate and improves ROE.
Execution of the announced ¥500 billion share buyback provides downside support and signals capital discipline.

key_risks: Gaming hardware decline and content-spend inflation
Image-sensor competition from Samsung, Omnivision, and Chinese suppliers
